evals.query: Set up a gRPC server in my Kora service that serves a UserService defined in a .proto file. Show me the dependencies and the handler.
expected_behavior: Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:grpc-server and io.grpc:grpc-protobuf plus the com.google.protobuf Gradle plugin with the proto srcDirs source sets
Includes the annotation processor: annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors" (or ksp "ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors" for Kotlin)
Extends GrpcServerModule on the @KoraApp interface
Implements a handler annotated @Component that extends the generated UserServiceGrpc.UserServiceImplBase and uses StreamObserver
Imports @Component from ru.tinkoff.kora.common.Component
Does not use field injection or non-Kora stereotype annotations (e.g. @GrpcService, @Service, @Autowired); uses constructor injection and @Component

evals.query: Where do I set the gRPC port and turn on request logging for the Kora gRPC server?
expected_behavior: Configures the grpcServer section with port (e.g. grpcServer.port)
Enables grpcServer.telemetry.logging.enabled = true (logging defaults to false)
Suggests externalizing the port via an environment variable such as ${GRPC_PORT}
Does not invent a foreign-framework server.port style property; uses the Kora grpcServer config section

evals.query: My gRPC handler should return a proper not-found error when the user does not exist instead of a generic failure.
expected_behavior: Uses io.grpc.Status.NOT_FOUND with withDescription(...).asRuntimeException()
Sends the error through responseObserver.onError(...) or throws the StatusRuntimeException
Explains that exactly one terminal call (onError or onCompleted) must be sent per RPC
Does not map the error to an HTTP status code or a foreign-framework response wrapper; stays gRPC-native with io.grpc.Status

evals.query: I want to test my Kora gRPC service with grpcurl without compiling proto stubs. How do I enable that?
expected_behavior: Adds the io.grpc:grpc-services dependency
Sets grpcServer.reflectionEnabled = true (notes the default is false)
Shows a grpcurl list command against the server port
Recommends keeping reflection disabled in production unless the endpoint is internal

evals.query: Add a server-streaming RPC GetAllUsers and a client-streaming RPC CreateUsers to my Kora gRPC handler.
expected_behavior: Server streaming: void getAllUsers(Empty, StreamObserver<UserResponse>) emitting multiple onNext then a single onCompleted
Client streaming: returns StreamObserver<CreateUserRequest> that accumulates onNext values and replies in onCompleted
Defines the rpc methods with the stream keyword in the .proto contract
Keeps business logic in an injected service component, not in the generated protobuf types
Does not introduce non-Kora streaming or controller abstractions; uses StreamObserver and @Component handlers

evals.query: Add API key authentication to my Kora gRPC server by checking the authorization metadata header.
expected_behavior: Implements io.grpc.ServerInterceptor annotated @Component so GrpcServerModule discovers it
Reads the header via Metadata.Key.of("authorization", Metadata.ASCII_STRING_MARSHALLER)
Rejects with call.close(Status.UNAUTHENTICATED, ...) and returns an empty ServerCall.Listener
Reads the expected key from a typed @ConfigSource interface injected as a component, not from a @ConfigSource-annotated constructor parameter
Does not use a servlet filter or foreign-framework security chain; authentication lives in a Kora ServerInterceptor @Component

evals.query: Which interceptors does the Kora gRPC server register by default, and how do I replace them?
expected_behavior: Lists the default interceptors: ContextServerInterceptor, CoroutineContextInjectInterceptor, MetricCollectorServerInterceptor, LoggingServerInterceptor
Explains that overriding the serverBuilder method from GrpcModule replaces the default list
Notes that a custom @Component implementing ServerInterceptor is added on top of the defaults
Does not reference foreign-framework interceptor APIs; stays with io.grpc.ServerInterceptor and GrpcModule

evals.query: What metrics does the Kora gRPC server emit and how do I add custom tags to them?
expected_behavior: Names rpc.server.duration, rpc.server.requests_per_rpc, and rpc.server.responses_per_rpc metrics
Configures grpcServer.telemetry.metrics.tags for custom metric tags and telemetry.tracing.attributes for span attributes
Notes metrics and tracing default to enabled while logging defaults to disabled
Does not suggest a separate actuator or @Timed annotation as the mechanism; the grpcServer.telemetry module emits the metrics automatically
